title: Doctor-Oriented Professional UI with Glass Effects & Dynamic Section Visibility

requirements.summary: Implement dynamic section visibility logic that hides empty sections completely
acceptanceCriteria: About section is hidden when bio text is empty and no profile image is set.
Clinics section is hidden when the clinics list is empty.
Services section is hidden when the services list is empty.
Social Media section is hidden when the social links list is empty.
Each section renders normally as soon as at least one piece of content is present.
No empty state message, skeleton, or placeholder is shown to public visitors.

file_operations.description: Add visibility logic to conditionally render About, Clinics, Services, and SocialMedia section components only when they contain content. Check aboutSection text and image URL for About, clinics array length for Clinics, services array length for Services, and socialLinks array length for SocialMedia. Omit the section component entirely from render when empty.
Remove any empty-state placeholders or skeleton UI. Ensure the component renders its full content structure only when it has data to display.
Remove any empty-state placeholders or skeleton UI. Ensure the component renders its clinic grid only when the clinics array has at least one entry.
Remove any empty-state placeholders or skeleton UI. Ensure the component renders its service grid only when the services array has at least one entry.
Remove any empty-state placeholders or skeleton UI. Ensure the component renders its social link grid only when the socialLinks array has at least one entry.

requirements.summary: Apply professional doctor-oriented design system with medical-grade aesthetics
acceptanceCriteria: Color palette reflects a medical/clinical aesthetic (no purely decorative neon overuse).
Typography hierarchy is clear with Space Grotesk headings and Inter body text.
All interactive elements have polished hover and focus states.
Layout feels structured, professional, and appropriate for a doctor's profile site.
The overall design communicates expertise and trust.

file_operations.description: Update the color palette to reflect a clean medical aesthetic with crisp white, deep charcoal, and trusted clinical accent colors (teal or soft emerald). Replace overly decorative neon tones with professional clinical tones. Ensure Space Grotesk is configured for headings and Inter for body text. Update hover and focus ring styles to be polished and refined.

Update CSS custom properties for light and dark themes to use the new medical-grade color palette. Define clinical accent tokens for borders, text highlights, and interactive states. Ensure all color variables align with the professional doctor-oriented aesthetic.

Update the hero section styling to use the new medical-grade design system with refined typography (Space Grotesk for heading, Inter for subtext), clinical accent colors, and polished spacing. Ensure buttons and interactive elements have professional hover/focus states.

Apply the updated typography hierarchy (Space Grotesk headings, Inter body), clinical color palette, and refined spacing. Ensure credential stat cards use professional styling appropriate for a medical profile.
Update clinic cards to use the professional medical design system with clinical accent colors cycling, refined typography, and polished hover states on interactive elements.
Apply the medical-grade design system to service cards with clinical accent colors, Space Grotesk for titles, Inter for descriptions, and polished hover/focus states.
Update social media cards to use the professional design system with clinical tones and refined typography, ensuring consistency with the overall medical aesthetic.
Apply the updated medical-grade design system to the footer with clinical accent colors, refined typography, and professional link hover states.
Update header navigation styling to use the clinical color palette, Space Grotesk for navigation labels, and polished hover/active states appropriate for a medical professional's site.

requirements.summary: Apply consistent glassmorphism effects across all cards, modals, navigation, and panels
acceptanceCriteria: GlassCard component is updated to use backdrop-blur and semi-transparent background.
Admin sidebar and panel surfaces use consistent glass styling.
Navigation header uses a frosted glass effect.
All glass surfaces maintain readable text contrast.
Glass effects are applied consistently — no mix of opaque and glass cards within the same section.

file_operations.description: Update GlassCard to use glassmorphism styling with backdrop-blur-md or backdrop-blur-lg, semi-transparent background (bg-opacity-10 to bg-opacity-20), subtle light border, and soft inner glow. Ensure text contrast remains readable on all backgrounds. Replace the current gradient background with the glass effect while maintaining the accent color border and optional hover lift.

Apply a frosted glass effect to the header with backdrop-blur, semi-transparent background, and subtle border. Ensure navigation text contrast is maintained for readability in both light and dark contexts.

Update the admin sidebar to use consistent glassmorphism styling with backdrop-blur, semi-transparent background, and subtle borders. Apply glass effects to the main content area panels as well. Ensure all text remains readable on glass surfaces.
Wrap editor content in glass-styled panels using backdrop-blur and semi-transparent backgrounds. Ensure input fields and text maintain readable contrast on glass surfaces.
Apply glassmorphism to clinic editor cards and forms using backdrop-blur and semi-transparent backgrounds. Ensure all form elements are readable.
Apply glassmorphism to service editor cards and forms using backdrop-blur and semi-transparent backgrounds. Ensure icon preview and text fields maintain readability.
Apply glassmorphism to social link editor cards using backdrop-blur and semi-transparent backgrounds. Ensure platform icons and text are readable.
Apply glassmorphism to the header editor panel using backdrop-blur and semi-transparent background.
Apply glassmorphism to the footer editor panel using backdrop-blur and semi-transparent background.
Apply glassmorphism to the hero settings editor panel using backdrop-blur and semi-transparent background. Ensure color picker and range inputs are readable.
Apply glassmorphism to the images manager panel using backdrop-blur and semi-transparent background. Ensure image previews and upload controls are clear.
Ensure ClinicCard uses the updated GlassCard component with glassmorphism effects. Verify that all clinic details remain readable on the glass surface.
Ensure stat cards or any card-based elements use the updated GlassCard with glassmorphism. Verify text readability.
Ensure service cards use the updated GlassCard with glassmorphism. Verify icon visibility and text readability on glass surfaces.
Ensure social media link cards use the updated GlassCard with glassmorphism. Verify icon visibility and text readability.

requirements.summary: Redesign AdminDashboard into a powerful, smart admin interface with grouped navigation, status indicators, and professional CMS-style controls
acceptanceCriteria: Admin sidebar has grouped navigation items with icons for each section.
Active section is clearly highlighted in the sidebar.
Each editor section shows a status badge indicating whether the section is empty or has content.
Editors use glass card containers with clear section titles and action buttons.
Save/cancel actions have clear visual states (loading, success, error).
The admin interface feels like a professional medical practice management tool.

file_operations.description: Restructure the sidebar navigation with grouped sections and icons for each navigation item (Hero, Images, Header, About, Clinics, Services, Social, Footer). Highlight the active section with a clear visual indicator (accent border, background, or glow). Add section health status badges next to each nav item showing whether the section is empty or has content (use data from getAllContent query). Apply glassmorphism to the sidebar as per REQ-3. Ensure the content area uses glass card containers for each editor.

Add a section status indicator showing whether the About section is empty or has content. Wrap the editor in a glass card container with a clear section title. Ensure save/cancel buttons have loading, success, and error visual states using appropriate UI feedback (spinners, checkmarks, error messages).

Add a section status indicator showing the number of clinics or empty state. Wrap the manager in a glass card container with a clear section title. Ensure add/edit/delete actions have clear visual states (loading, success, error) with appropriate feedback.

Add a section status indicator showing the number of services or empty state. Wrap the manager in a glass card container with a clear section title. Ensure add/edit/delete actions have clear visual states (loading, success, error) with appropriate feedback.

Add a section status indicator showing the number of social links or empty state. Wrap the manager in a glass card container with a clear section title. Ensure add/edit/delete actions have clear visual states (loading, success, error) with appropriate feedback.

Add a section status indicator showing whether the header has content. Wrap the editor in a glass card container with a clear section title. Ensure save actions have clear visual states (loading, success, error).

Add a section status indicator showing whether the footer has content. Wrap the editor in a glass card container with a clear section title. Ensure save actions have clear visual states (loading, success, error).
Wrap the editor in a glass card container with a clear section title. Ensure save/reset actions have clear visual states (loading, success, error) with appropriate feedback.

Add a section status indicator showing whether the hero background image is set. Wrap the manager in a glass card container with a clear section title. Ensure upload/delete actions have clear visual states (loading, success, error).
